Fetch acceptance: `mcp__ledger__fetch_item` ledger=tasks item=T121.

Diff: `git -C /home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia diff 74f38b7 a9488e46a778f0a155d9f0d00539c9f57bde3f94`.

CONTEXT: YOU found the round-1 blocking defects by EXECUTING the test. Round 2 claims to fix all three:
- FIX 1: r121PeerCounter now reads the single-peer NO-label exposition via PeerValue-then-Value fallback (was exp.PeerValue(name,"") which single-peer daemons never emit), inverted comment corrected.
- FIX 2: the skip-gate now probes /dev/net/tun FIRST so unprivileged runs SKIP (was: dummy-link probe passes in the userns, then FAIL at CreateTUN).
- FIX 3: the post-restart check now polls the restarted process's 'tunnel interface up' log record for genuine re-adoption (was: vacuous waitLink under tun_persist).

Verify EACH fix resolves YOUR criticism: (1) the counter helper now reads the single-peer exposition correctly (exp.Value fallback) so the D36 rebaselines/dropSuspect assertions can actually observe values — trace against internal/metrics multiPeer logic; (2) RE-RUN the unprivileged test yourself: `nix develop -c sh -c 'go test -tags e2e ./test/e2e -run TestOneSidedRestartRecovery -v 2>&1 | tail -20'` and confirm it now `---SKIP`s (not FAILs) at the /dev/net/tun probe BEFORE any daemon bring-up; (3) the re-adoption check is now non-vacuous (asserts the actual 'tunnel interface up' record, not the persisted link); (4) nothing new broke — build/vet -tags e2e clean, just lint 0, just test green, port 9104 still unique+registered. Run `cd /home/pavel/work/safe/fecalia/.claude/worktrees/agent-a8df19fee473f30bd && nix develop -c sh -c 'go build ./... && go build -tags e2e ./test/e2e/... && go vet -tags e2e ./test/e2e/...' && nix develop -c just lint && nix develop -c just test`. If all three fixes hold and the test genuinely SKIPS unprivileged, verdict approve. Split criticism[] vs questions[]; file genuine defects.

attachment.content:
- baboon: Baboon data model compiler reference. Covers model syntax, multi-version evolution, code generation, wire-format conversions, database migrations, backward compatibility, and version negotiation. TRIGGER when: designing, changing, or discussing any external API, working with .baboon model files, updating data models, adding new fields or types, bumping model versions, running code generation, implementing conversions, writing database migrations, or discussing API versioning strategy.
- constructive-test-taxonomy: Multi-axis classification of tests (Intention × Encapsulation × Isolation) for evaluating test quality and steering test-writing decisions. Replaces fuzzy Unit/Integration/E2E vocabulary with precise tags and a maintenance-cost heuristic. TRIGGER when: writing new tests, reviewing a test suite, deciding what to test or at what level, discussing mocks vs. real dependencies, classifying tests, or arguing about test coverage or test-strategy tradeoffs.
- dual-tests: Pattern for testing code that touches external systems: write one abstract test suite against an interface, then run it against both the production adapter (real DB/API/FS) and a hand-written in-memory dummy. Prefers manual dummies over auto-generated mocks. TRIGGER when: designing code that depends on a database, HTTP API, message queue, filesystem, or other external system; choosing between mocks, fakes, or dummies; adding integration points; setting up test infrastructure; or when the `constructive-test-taxonomy` skill calls for moving Communication-tier tests toward Blackbox-Atomic.
- environment: Bubblewrap "yolo" sandbox escape workflow (exchange scripts for reaching $HOME, system paths, or files outside the sandbox) plus in-sandbox nix/direnv usage. TRIGGER only when $SMIND_SANDBOXED=1 — i.e. running inside the yolo sandbox and you need to reach something outside it. SKIP entirely when $SMIND_SANDBOXED is unset: you are unsandboxed, so write directly and ignore this skill.
- izumi: Best-practice playbook for the izumi Scala ecosystem — distage (DI), distage-framework (roles/RoleAppMain), distage-testkit (test harness with memoization), distage-framework-docker (test containers), logstage (structured logging), and BIO (bifunctor effect typeclasses). Covers idiomatic bindings, Activation/Axis switching, Lifecycle discipline, plugin layout, and how to apply the `dual-tests` pattern inside distage so one test body runs against both a dummy and a real-DB adapter. TRIGGER when: writing or reviewing Scala code that imports `izumi.distage.*`, `izumi.logstage.*`, `izumi.functional.bio.*`, `distage.*`, `logstage.*`; designing a new distage plugin or Role; wiring a testkit `SpecZIO`/`Spec2`/`Spec1`; configuring `ContainerDef`/`DockerSupportModule`; choosing between `StandardAxis.Repo` or `Scene` tags; or implementing the dual-tests pattern in a distage project. The canonical reference project is https://github.com/7mind/distage-example.
- resilient-ws-ui: Guidelines for building applications that maintain reliable WebSocket connections to a backend and surface connection health clearly to the user. Invoke ONLY when the user explicitly references this skill by name (e.g. "/resilient-ws-ui", "use the resilient-ws-ui skill"). Do NOT auto-invoke based on topic match.
- tass: Target/Actual State Separation (TASS) pattern for reliable state management in event-driven control systems. TRIGGER when: implementing device or entity state management, designing control systems, working with target vs actual state separation, or building event-driven systems with commanded/observed state.
- cq:advance: Advance the WHOLE flow one cycle — chain /cq:investigate:advance, /cq:plan:advance, /cq:implement:advance to quiescence, then report DRAINED / BLOCKED-ON-QUESTIONS / MIXED.
- cq:implement-review: Implement-flow adversarial per-task review rubric + stdout-JSON contract, as a portable promptTemplate for non-Claude harnesses (pi/Codex). Symmetric to cq:plan-review. Judges ONE task's implementation, splits findings into autonomously-fixable criticism[], user-only questions[], and out-of-scope defects[], and emits the verdict as JSON on stdout. Writes NOTHING to the ledger — the orchestrator records the terminal review.
- cq:implement:advance: Advance an implement-flow run one full pass — pick DAG-ready tasks, implement each in an isolated worktree, adversarially review, autonomously fix criticism, register user questions, and merge back in dependency order.
- cq:implement:start: Start an implement-flow run — resolve the target milestones (default: all open ones), validate their task DAG, then hand off to the /cq:implement:advance loop.
- cq:investigate: Start an investigate-flow run — defect intake + bootstrap, then run the /cq:investigate:advance pass inline.
- cq:investigate:advance: Advance an investigate-flow run one research round — read defect state, form/extend the hypothesis tree, dispatch read-only explorers, validate every citation against source, adjudicate node status, and on a confirmed root cause file-and-defer the fix to plan-flow.
- cq:plan: Start a plan-flow goal — create the goal (from free text OR one-or-more idea-ids, one goal per idea), then hand off to the planner for the first clarifying questions.
- cq:plan-review: Shared adversarial plan-review rubric — judge an emitted plan for a goal against the fine-grained / sequenced / testable / grounded / complete rubric, classify findings into new_questions / criticism / defects, and emit a single fenced-json verdict on stdout. The CANONICAL rubric source; Claude's plan-reviewer agent and every non-Claude (Codex/Pi) reviewer judge the same way and emit the same JSON.
- cq:plan:advance: Advance plan-flow goals one full round — a given goal, or (no argument) every unlocked goal — running the planner↔reviewer loop until each needs the user or reaches `planned`.
- cq:plan:follow-up: Add new scope to an EXISTING plan-flow goal — append the follow-up request, re-open the goal, and hand to the planner for a fresh clarifying round.
- cq:planners: Session-only planner-set override — parse a natural-language planner instruction (e.g. "use grok and opus only") into canonical harness:model tokens via the cq.toml [aliases] table (from the ledger MCP get_planners/get_config) plus a documented fallback map; echo the resolved active set; confirm the override is SESSION-ONLY and reverts on the next fresh run.
- cq:reviewers: Session-only reviewer-set override — parse a natural-language reviewer instruction (e.g. "use grok and opus only") into canonical harness:model tokens via the cq.toml [aliases] table (from the ledger MCP get_reviewers/get_config) plus a documented fallback map; echo the resolved active set; confirm the override is SESSION-ONLY and reverts on the next fresh run.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- codex:rescue: Delegate investigation, an explicit fix request, or follow-up rescue work to the Codex rescue subagent
- codex:setup: Check whether the local Codex CLI is ready and optionally toggle the stop-time review gate
- codex:codex-cli-runtime: Internal helper contract for calling the codex-companion runtime from Claude Code
- codex:codex-result-handling: Internal guidance for presenting Codex helper output back to the user
- codex:gpt-5-4-prompting: Internal guidance for composing Codex and GPT-5.4 prompts for coding, review, diagnosis, and research tasks inside the Codex Claude Code plugin
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

78	//	     HOME=/root GOTOOLCHAIN=local GOCACHE=/tmp/gocache \
79	//	     go test -tags e2e ./test/e2e -run TestOneSidedRestartRecovery -v'
80	const (
81		// r121EdgeMetrics is the EDGE's /metrics listen (loopback — the edge runs in the base
82		// test-process netns, so 127.0.0.1 is directly scrapeable). The concentrator runs in the
83		// PEER netns where a 127.0.0.1 listener is unreachable from the base netns, so it binds
84		// its primary uplink address instead (r121ConcMetricsListen, reachable over the veth).
85		// Both use the SAME new port 9104 (distinct addresses/netns — no collision); 9104 is this
86		// file's registry entry (see netns.go metricsPortRegistry).
87		r121MetricsPort    = 9104
88		r121EdgeMetrics    = "127.0.0.1:9104"
89		r121EdgeMetricsURL = "http://" + r121EdgeMetrics + "/metrics"
90	
91		// r121ReconvergeBudget bounds one-sided-restart reconvergence (session_established
92		// 0->1). It sits WELL under WG's own rescue timers and targets the ~25s both-ends-fresh
93		// baseline, with headroom so a slow CI host does not flake. The measured per-direction
94		// magnitude is LOGGED for the D36 record (edge-restart predicted ~10s).
95		r121ReconvergeBudget = 30 * time.Second
96	
97		// r121D37Budget bounds cold-start time-to-first-handshake measured from the first
98		// path-up edge: it must track path-up (+~1 RTT), NOT a 5s WG retransmit-timer multiple
99		// (the D37 symptom). Strictly below one 5s retransmit so a regression to the retransmit
100		// path (>=5s) fails the assertion.
101		r121D37Budget = 4 * time.Second
102	
103		// r121SatSeconds is the saturating-flow duration: long enough to push the surviving
104		// side's resequencer `next` WELL past resequencerWindow (2048) frames — the D36
105		// precondition, without which the restarted peer's outer-seq-~1 frames would admit
106		// normally and the SUSPECT-drop pathology would never arise.
107		r121SatSeconds = 6
108	
109		// r121ReseqWindow mirrors internal/bind.resequencerWindow (2048, unexported): the
110		// precondition asserts the survivor released MORE than one window of frames, so its
111		// release point `next` is provably past the window before the restart.
112		r121ReseqWindow = 2048
113	
114		// r121DropSuspectSlack tolerates at most a few genuine stale-high reorder stragglers
115		// being SUSPECT-dropped around the restart (which T119's low-anchor re-anchor drops
116		// CORRECTLY). It is far below the dozens-to-hundreds the D36 pathology would drop while
117		// every wrapped-init retransmit is suspect-dropped over the multi-minute outage, so it
118		// still catches the defect while not flaking on bounded cross-path reorder.
119		r121DropSuspectSlack = 3
120	)

122	// TestOneSidedRestartRecovery runs both D36/D37 restart directions (run A: restart only
123	// the edge; run B: restart only the concentrator), each on its own fresh topology.
124	func TestOneSidedRestartRecovery(t *testing.T) {
125		requireNetAdmin(t)
126		bin := buildWanbond(t)
127	
128		t.Run("restart-only-edge", func(t *testing.T) {
129			testRestartOnlyEdge(t, bin)
130		})
131		t.Run("restart-only-concentrator", func(t *testing.T) {
132			testRestartOnlyConcentrator(t, bin)
133		})
134	}
135	
136	// testRestartOnlyEdge is run A: the concentrator is the SURVIVOR. The edge saturates the
137	// bond (edge->conc), advancing the concentrator's per-peer resequencer `next` past the
138	// window; then ONLY the edge process restarts. The concentrator must re-anchor (rebaseline)
139	// on the edge's authenticated restart epoch and admit the wrapped init instead of
140	// suspect-dropping it, and the edge's session_established must return 0->1 within budget.
141	func testRestartOnlyEdge(t *testing.T, bin string) {
142		t.Helper()
143		top := SetupWithPaths(t, DefaultPaths)
144		primary := DefaultPaths[primaryPathIdx] // starlink — the concentrator uplink the edge targets
145		survivorURL := "http://" + primary.concIP + ":" + strconv.Itoa(r121MetricsPort) + "/metrics"
146	
147		edge, conc, edgeArgv, _ := r121BringUp(t, top, bin, DefaultPaths, primary.concIP)
148	
149		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
150			t.Fatalf("bond never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", edge.log(), conc.log())
151		}
152		// (1) initial convergence: the restarted side we will re-assert is the edge; confirm it
153		// established at all before the restart so the post-restart 0->1 is a genuine transition.
154		waitSessionEstablished(t, r121EdgeMetricsURL, r121ReconvergeBudget)
155		// (3) D37 cold-start first-handshake, from the edge log at initial bring-up.
156		assertD37FirstHandshake(t, edge)
157	
158		// D36 precondition: saturate edge->conc so the CONCENTRATOR resequencer `next` advances
159		// well past the window. iperf3 client on the edge, one-shot server in the peer netns.
160		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-server", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "iperf3", "-s", "-1", "-B", concInner)
161		time.Sleep(400 * time.Millisecond)
162		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-load", "iperf3", "-c", concInner, "-t", strconv.Itoa(r121SatSeconds))
163		time.Sleep(time.Duration(r121SatSeconds+1) * time.Second) // let the flow run to completion + queues drain
164	
165		before := scrapeMetrics(t, survivorURL)
166		relBefore := r121PeerCounter(t, before, metrics.MetricReseqReleased, "concentrator survivor")
167		if relBefore <= r121ReseqWindow {
168			t.Fatalf("D36 precondition unmet: concentrator survivor released only %.0f frames (<= window %d) — `next` did not advance past the window, so a restart could not exercise the SUSPECT-drop path",
169				relBefore, r121ReseqWindow)
170		}
171		rebaseBefore := r121PeerCounter(t, before, metrics.MetricReseqRebaselines, "concentrator survivor")
172		dropBefore := r121PeerCounter(t, before, metrics.MetricReseqDroppedSuspect, "concentrator survivor")
173	
174		// RESTART ONLY THE EDGE (tun_persist keeps wanbond0 + its inner addr across the stop).
175		restartAt := time.Now()
176		top.stopAndWait(t, edge)
177		edge = top.startProc(t, "edge", edgeArgv...)
178		if !r121WaitReadopted(t, edge, 5*time.Second) {
179			t.Fatalf("restarted edge did not re-adopt %s: no 'tunnel interface up' record in the restarted process log within 5s\n%s", tunDev, edge.log())
180		}
181	
182		// (1) the restarted edge's session_established returns 0->1 within budget.
183		waitSessionEstablished(t, r121EdgeMetricsURL, r121ReconvergeBudget)
184		reconv := time.Since(restartAt)
185		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, r121ReconvergeBudget) {
186			t.Fatalf("tunnel did not carry inner traffic within %s of the edge restart\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s",
187				r121ReconvergeBudget, edge.log(), conc.log())
188		}
189	
190		// (2) the SURVIVING concentrator re-anchored (rebaselines>=1) and did NOT suspect-drop
191		// the wrapped init (~0 dropSuspect delta).
192		after := scrapeMetrics(t, survivorURL)
193		rebaseAfter := r121PeerCounter(t, after, metrics.MetricReseqRebaselines, "concentrator survivor")
194		dropAfter := r121PeerCounter(t, after, metrics.MetricReseqDroppedSuspect, "concentrator survivor")
195		r121AssertRecovery(t, "edge-restart", reconv, rebaseBefore, rebaseAfter, dropBefore, dropAfter, relBefore)
196	}

198	// testRestartOnlyConcentrator is run B: the edge is the SURVIVOR. The concentrator
199	// saturates the bond in REVERSE (conc->edge via iperf3 -R), advancing the edge's per-peer
200	// resequencer `next` past the window; then ONLY the concentrator process restarts (no
201	// failover — the concentrator role runs none). The edge must re-anchor on the
202	// concentrator's authenticated restart epoch, and the concentrator's session_established
203	// must return 0->1 within budget.
204	func testRestartOnlyConcentrator(t *testing.T, bin string) {
205		t.Helper()
206		top := SetupWithPaths(t, DefaultPaths)
207		primary := DefaultPaths[primaryPathIdx] // starlink
208		restartedURL := "http://" + primary.concIP + ":" + strconv.Itoa(r121MetricsPort) + "/metrics"
209	
210		edge, conc, _, concArgv := r121BringUp(t, top, bin, DefaultPaths, primary.concIP)
211	
212		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, 15*time.Second) {
213			t.Fatalf("bond never came up\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s", edge.log(), conc.log())
214		}
215		waitSessionEstablished(t, restartedURL, r121ReconvergeBudget)
216		// (3) D37 cold-start first-handshake is an EDGE property; assert it on the edge log at
217		// initial bring-up (the concentrator is the responder and initiates nothing).
218		assertD37FirstHandshake(t, edge)
219	
220		// D36 precondition: saturate conc->edge (reverse) so the EDGE resequencer `next` advances
221		// past the window. The one-shot server runs in the peer netns; iperf3 -R makes the SERVER
222		// (concentrator side) send to the client (edge), so the edge is the receiver.
223		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-server", "nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", "iperf3", "-s", "-1", "-B", concInner)
224		time.Sleep(400 * time.Millisecond)
225		top.startProc(t, "iperf3-load", "iperf3", "-c", concInner, "-R", "-t", strconv.Itoa(r121SatSeconds))
226		time.Sleep(time.Duration(r121SatSeconds+1) * time.Second)
227	
228		before := scrapeMetrics(t, r121EdgeMetricsURL)
229		relBefore := r121PeerCounter(t, before, metrics.MetricReseqReleased, "edge survivor")
230		if relBefore <= r121ReseqWindow {
231			t.Fatalf("D36 precondition unmet: edge survivor released only %.0f frames (<= window %d) — `next` did not advance past the window",
232				relBefore, r121ReseqWindow)
233		}
234		rebaseBefore := r121PeerCounter(t, before, metrics.MetricReseqRebaselines, "edge survivor")
235		dropBefore := r121PeerCounter(t, before, metrics.MetricReseqDroppedSuspect, "edge survivor")
236	
237		// RESTART ONLY THE CONCENTRATOR (tun_persist keeps the peer-netns wanbond0 + concInner).
238		restartAt := time.Now()
239		top.stopAndWait(t, conc)
240		conc = top.startProc(t, "concentrator", concArgv...)
241		if !r121WaitReadopted(t, conc, 5*time.Second) {
242			t.Fatalf("restarted concentrator did not re-adopt %s: no 'tunnel interface up' record in the restarted process log within 5s\n%s", tunDev, conc.log())
243		}
244	
245		// (1) the restarted concentrator's session_established returns 0->1 within budget.
246		waitSessionEstablished(t, restartedURL, r121ReconvergeBudget)
247		reconv := time.Since(restartAt)
248		if !top.pingUntil(concInner, r121ReconvergeBudget) {
249			t.Fatalf("tunnel did not carry inner traffic within %s of the concentrator restart\n--- edge ---\n%s\n--- conc ---\n%s",
250				r121ReconvergeBudget, edge.log(), conc.log())
251		}
252	
253		// (2) the SURVIVING edge re-anchored and did NOT suspect-drop the wrapped init.
254		after := scrapeMetrics(t, r121EdgeMetricsURL)
255		rebaseAfter := r121PeerCounter(t, after, metrics.MetricReseqRebaselines, "edge survivor")
256		dropAfter := r121PeerCounter(t, after, metrics.MetricReseqDroppedSuspect, "edge survivor")
257		r121AssertRecovery(t, "concentrator-restart", reconv, rebaseBefore, rebaseAfter, dropBefore, dropAfter, relBefore)
258	}

260	// r121AssertRecovery is the shared (2) assertion + D36-record logging for both directions:
261	// the survivor rebaselined at least once and did not suspect-drop the wrapped init.
262	func r121AssertRecovery(t *testing.T, dir string, reconv time.Duration, rebaseBefore, rebaseAfter, dropBefore, dropAfter, released float64) {
263		t.Helper()
264		rebaseDelta := rebaseAfter - rebaseBefore
265		dropDelta := dropAfter - dropBefore
266		// Capture the counters + reconvergence magnitude in the test output for the D36 record.
267		t.Logf("D36/%s: session_established 0->1 in %s (budget %s); survivor released=%.0f (> window %d) "+
268			"rebaselines %.0f->%.0f (delta %.0f) dropSuspect %.0f->%.0f (delta %.0f)",
269			dir, reconv.Round(time.Millisecond), r121ReconvergeBudget, released, r121ReseqWindow,
270			rebaseBefore, rebaseAfter, rebaseDelta, dropBefore, dropAfter, dropDelta)
271		if rebaseDelta < 1 {
272			t.Errorf("D36/%s: survivor rebaselines delta = %.0f, want >= 1 — the authenticated peer-restart epoch change did "+
273				"not re-anchor the survivor's resequencer, so the restarted peer's low-outer-seq wrapped init would be "+
274				"SUSPECT-dropped (the D36 multi-minute-outage pathology). T119 wires this via dispatchInbound's "+
275				"RebaselineToLow on epochChanged.", dir, rebaseDelta)
276		}
277		if dropDelta > r121DropSuspectSlack {
278			t.Errorf("D36/%s: survivor dropSuspect delta = %.0f across the restart, want ~0 (<= %d slack for stale-high "+
279				"reorder stragglers) — the wrapped WG init/response was SUSPECT-dropped rather than re-anchored, which is "+
280				"exactly the D36 defect: recovery then waits on a WG rescue timer instead of the trusted re-anchor.",
281				dir, dropDelta, r121DropSuspectSlack)
282		}
283	}

285	// assertD37FirstHandshake is the (3) D37 assertion: from cold start, the edge's first WG
286	// handshake tracks the first path-up edge (+~1 RTT), NOT a 5s WG retransmit-timer multiple.
287	// It reads the edge log timestamps (INFO 'path liveness transition' to=up and 'session
288	// established'), the same evidence TestSessionEstablishedTransitions uses.
289	func assertD37FirstHandshake(t *testing.T, edge *proc) {
290		t.Helper()
291		logText := edge.log()
292		pathUpAt, ok := pathLivenessUpTime(logText)
293		if !ok {
294			t.Fatalf("D37: no 'path liveness transition' to=up record in the edge log\n%s", logText)
295		}
296		sessionAt, ok := logRecordTime(logText, "session established")
297		if !ok {
298			t.Fatalf("D37: no 'session established' record in the edge log\n%s", logText)
299		}
300		if sessionAt.Before(pathUpAt) {
301			t.Errorf("D37: session established (%s) preceded path up (%s); expected path up FIRST",
302				sessionAt.Format(time.RFC3339Nano), pathUpAt.Format(time.RFC3339Nano))
303		}
304		delta := sessionAt.Sub(pathUpAt)
305		t.Logf("D37 cold start: first handshake %s after the first path-up edge (budget %s)", delta.Round(time.Millisecond), r121D37Budget)
306		if delta > r121D37Budget {
307			t.Errorf("D37: cold-start time-to-first-handshake = %s after path-up, want <= %s (~1 RTT) — a value tracking a 5s "+
308				"WG retransmit-timer multiple means the first init fired pre-liveness and was NOT re-driven off the path-up "+
309				"edge (the D37 symptom). T120 drives a forced SendHandshakeInitiation off the first path-up edge.",
310				delta, r121D37Budget)
311		}
312	}

314	// r121BringUp starts the concentrator (peer netns, via nsenter) and the edge (base netns),
315	// waits for both wanbond0 TUNs, plumbs the inner overlay addresses, and returns both procs
316	// plus the exact argv each was started with (so a restart re-launches an identical process).
317	// Both ends run tun_persist=true so the inner address survives a full daemon stop/start, and
318	// at [log] level "info" so the D37 path-up / session-established records are emitted. The
319	// edge binds its /metrics on loopback; the concentrator binds its primary uplink address so
320	// the base netns can scrape it over the veth.
321	func r121BringUp(t *testing.T, top *Topology, bin string, paths []pathSpec, concMetricsIP string) (edge, conc *proc, edgeArgv, concArgv []string) {
322		t.Helper()
323	
324		edgePriv, edgePub := genKey(t)
325		concPriv, concPub := genKey(t)
326		psk := randKey(t)
327	
328		concMetricsListen := concMetricsIP + ":" + strconv.Itoa(r121MetricsPort)
329	
330		dir := t.TempDir()
331		edgeCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "edge.toml"),
332			r121EdgeConfig(psk, edgePriv, concPub, paths, r121EdgeMetrics))
333		concCfg := writeConfig(t, filepath.Join(dir, "conc.toml"),
334			r121ConcConfig(psk, concPriv, edgePub, paths, concMetricsListen))
335	
336		edgeArgv = []string{bin, "--config", edgeCfg}
337		concArgv = []string{"nsenter", "-t", strconv.Itoa(top.pid), "-n", bin, "--config", concCfg}
338	
339		conc = top.startProc(t, "concentrator", concArgv...)
340		edge = top.startProc(t, "edge", edgeArgv...)
341	
342		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, false, 5*time.Second) {
343			t.Fatalf("edge %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, edge.log())
344		}
345		if !top.waitLink(tunDev, true, 5*time.Second) {
346			t.Fatalf("concentrator %s never appeared\n%s", tunDev, conc.log())
347		}
348		top.run("ip", "addr", "add", edgeInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
349		top.run("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
350		top.nsenter("ip", "addr", "add", concInner+"/24", "dev", tunDev)
351		top.nsenter("ip", "link", "set", tunDev, "up")
352		return edge, conc, edgeArgv, concArgv
353	}

410	// r121PeerCounter reads a resequencer counter for the survivor's sole peer. Because each
411	// daemon binds EXACTLY ONE peer, metrics.NewCollector sets multiPeer = len(PeerNames())>1
412	// = FALSE (internal/metrics/metrics.go:260-266), so the reseq series are emitted UNLABELED
413	// — the value lives in the no-label exposition read by Value(name), exactly as
414	// internal/metrics' TestExpositionReseqRebaselineAndDropSuspect asserts. A `peer=""` LABEL
415	// exists ONLY in a MULTI-peer exposition's unnamed-primary series (BoundPeerNames' primary
416	// override), which is NOT present here — so PeerValue(name, "") returns (0,false) for these
417	// single-peer daemons. We try PeerValue(name, "") first so the helper also works against a
418	// multi-peer exposition, then fall back to the single-peer no-label series. Missing is
419	// treated as 0 for a rebaseline/drop counter (a rebaseline/drop that never happened yields
420	// no series until the first increment); the caller's deltas remain correct. `released` must
421	// be present once traffic flowed, so its absence is surfaced as a real fault rather than
422	// silently read as 0.
423	func r121PeerCounter(t *testing.T, exp metrics.Exposition, name, who string) float64 {
424		t.Helper()
425		if v, ok := exp.PeerValue(name, ""); ok {
426			return v
427		}
428		if v, ok := exp.Value(name); ok {
429			return v
430		}
431		if name == metrics.MetricReseqReleased {
432			t.Fatalf("%s absent from the %s /metrics after traffic flowed (checked both the peer=\"\" multi-peer series and the single-peer no-label exposition)", name, who)
433		}
434		return 0
435	}

437	// r121WaitReadopted asserts the RESTARTED process actually re-opened the persistent TUN,
438	// polling its OWN post-restart log for the 'tunnel interface up' record (cmd/wanbond/main.go).
439	// Under tun_persist=true the kernel link SURVIVES the stop, so a plain `ip link show`
440	// (top.waitLink) is VACUOUS here — it passes whether or not the new process adopted the
441	// device, yet round 1's message claimed 're-adopted'. device.Up emits 'tunnel interface up'
442	// only AFTER CreateTUN's TUNSETIFF successfully (re-)adopts wanbond0 BY NAME
443	// (internal/device/persist_linux.go). p.log() is the fresh post-restart buffer (startProc
444	// returns a NEW proc with its own output), so this record proves re-adoption by THIS process,
445	// not a stale persistent link left by its predecessor.
446	func r121WaitReadopted(t *testing.T, p *proc, timeout time.Duration) bool {
447		t.Helper()
448		deadline := time.Now().Add(timeout)
449		for time.Now().Before(deadline) {
450			if _, ok := logRecordTime(p.log(), "tunnel interface up"); ok {
451				return true
452			}
453			time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
454		}
455		return false
456	}

458	// requireNetAdmin skips (does NOT fail) when this process cannot build the netns fixture and
459	// bring the daemons up — so `go test -tags e2e` in an unprivileged environment SKIPS this
460	// scenario rather than failing it. Under the privileged hardware suite (sudo / the TestMain
461	// user+net namespace) both probes succeed and the test runs.
462	//
463	// It probes TWO independent capabilities, /dev/net/tun FIRST:
464	//
465	//   - /dev/net/tun must exist: device.Up calls CreateTUN (TUNSETIFF) and hard-fails without
466	//     it. TestMain's `unshare -Urmn` grants CAP_NET_ADMIN inside the userns, so the dummy-link
467	//     probe below SUCCEEDS there even when the sandbox has NO /dev/net/tun — in which case both
468	//     subtests would otherwise FAIL ~5s later at daemon bring-up ("CreateTUN(wanbond0) failed").
469	//     Probing the tun device up front makes an unprivileged run SKIP BEFORE any daemon comes up.
470	//   - CAP_NET_ADMIN to create the veth/netem topology, probed by adding+deleting a dummy link.
471	func requireNetAdmin(t *testing.T) {
472		t.Helper()
473		if _, err := os.Stat("/dev/net/tun"); err != nil {
474			t.Skipf("/dev/net/tun unavailable (%v) — device.Up's CreateTUN(wanbond0) would fail; the one-sided-restart e2e requires the privileged suite; skipping", err)
475		}
476		const probe = "wbR121probe"
477		if out, err := exec.Command("ip", "link", "add", probe, "type", "dummy").CombinedOutput(); err != nil {
478			t.Skipf("no CAP_NET_ADMIN to build the netns fixture (%v: %s) — the one-sided-restart e2e requires the privileged suite; skipping",
479				err, strings.TrimSpace(string(out)))
480		}
481		_ = exec.Command("ip", "link", "del", probe).Run()

content.text:
All verification complete. Docs do not enumerate individual e2e scenarios or ports (the port registry lives in netns.go and was updated), so no doc-sync obligation attaches to this test-only addition.

### Verification summary against my round-1 criticisms

**FIX 1 (counter helper reads single-peer exposition)** — verified by tracing `internal/metrics/metrics.go`: `NewCollector` sets `multiPeer = len(src.PeerNames()) > 1`; both daemon configs bind exactly one `[[wireguard.peers]]`, so `peerLabelValues` returns `nil` and all reseq series are emitted **unlabeled** (metrics.go:382-387, Collect:356-364). In `scrape.go`, `PeerValue(name,"")` → `labeledValue` iterates label pairs — an unlabeled series has none, so it returns `(0,false)`; the new `Value(name)` fallback matches `len(m.GetLabel())==0` and reads the actual series. The helper fatals on a missing `released` (must exist post-traffic) and treats missing rebaseline/dropSuspect as 0 — deltas remain correct. The round-1 inverted comment is replaced by an accurate one citing the exact mechanism.

**FIX 2 (skip-gate)** — verified by **execution** with `-count=1` (uncached): `--- SKIP: TestOneSidedRestartRecovery (0.00s)` at restart_onesided_test.go:125, `/dev/net/tun unavailable`, before any daemon bring-up. The tun probe now precedes the dummy-link probe (requireNetAdmin:473-481), exactly closing the round-1 userns FAIL path.

**FIX 3 (non-vacuous re-adoption check)** — `r121WaitReadopted` polls the restarted process's **own** log for `tunnel interface up`, which cmd/wanbond/main.go:64 emits only after `device.Up` (hence `CreateTUN`/TUNSETIFF) returns successfully; `startProc` (p0_test.go:192) allocates a fresh `lockedBuffer` per proc, so the record provably comes from the new process, and `stopAndWait` fully reaps the predecessor first. The vacuous `waitLink` is gone from the restart paths.

**Regression gates** — all executed in the worktree: `go build ./... && go build -tags e2e && go vet -tags e2e` clean; `just lint` 0 issues across all three tag sets; `just test` 13 packages ok, zero failures; port 9104 unique in test/e2e and registered in netns.go's registry comment; file carries `//go:build e2e` so the default suite excludes it. Merge-base of a9488e4 with main == 74f38b7 as declared; diff is purely additive (483 lines, 2 files).

Acceptance clauses all hold operationally: run-A/run-B matrix encoded, reconvergence budget (30s « 180s WG rescue), rebaselines>=1 + dropSuspect<=3-slack deltas, D37 4s-budget (< one 5s retransmit) from log timestamps, runbook with exact `ssh -F none -i /run/agenix/llm-ssh-key` + sudo-PATH invocations for both hosts, privileged execution explicitly deferred.
